name: Intel's Fall Developer Forum (IDF): Megahertz is Dead, Long Live Dual-Core (Revised)
description: Insight 64 analyst report by Nathan Brookwood on Intel Fall 2004 IDF. Paul Otellini officially closed two decades of MHz-driven CPU scaling and announced the shift to multi-core parallelism. Analyzes the NetBurst power/performance trend, Pentium M Dothan (2GHz/21W, 140M transistors) versus Prescott (3.2GHz/82W, 125M) as exemplars of architecture over brute-force frequency, and the competitive bus-bandwidth disadvantage (3.2GB/s versus AMD's 13.2GB/s) if Intel used an inelegant dual-core design. Preserved in the Kastner archive as external reference material.
